Blog/Marketing Reflection
Marketing Reflection — BUSM 304

The Cola Wars Taught Me More About Marketing Than I Expected

Meredith T. Villaluz Spring 20264 min read

This week in my marketing class at Brigham Young University–Hawaii, I watched the History Channel's Cola Wars documentary — and it completely changed how I think about everyday purchasing decisions. Two nearly identical products locked in a billion-dollar battle. The real advantage? Understanding the consumer.

We Recreated the Pepsi Challenge

Our class recreated the famous Pepsi Challenge blind taste test — the same campaign that challenged Coca-Cola's dominance in the 1970s. Before the test, we were each asked which cola we preferred. As a proud Coca-Cola fan, my answer was easy: Coke, no hesitation.

Then came the real challenge: could I actually tell the difference without seeing the label? I could. And apparently so could most of my class.

Our Classroom Cola War Results
14
Votes for Coca-Cola
10
Votes for Pepsi
🏆
Coke Wins!

The Marketing Lesson Behind the Taste

But this is where it gets interesting from a marketing perspective. This exercise clearly showed the consumer decision-making process from Chapter 5. Even in a blind test, our brand loyalty, emotions, and perceptions influenced how we tasted and judged each drink. We weren't just evaluating flavor — we were evaluating everything we associate with that brand.

This is exactly what the documentary showed on a larger scale. Pepsi succeeded in market segmentation by targeting younger consumers with bold celebrity endorsements like Michael Jackson and creative advertising. Meanwhile, Coca-Cola focused on tradition and emotional storytelling to keep its loyal customers. Two brands, two very different positioning strategies — but the same brown fizzy drink.

"Great marketing isn't just about the product — it's about the relationship you build with your target market. Emotions, culture, and identity influence purchase decisions much more than features or even taste."

I'm grateful to study these real-world applications at BYUH, where learning goes beyond the textbook. Are you Team Coke or Team Pepsi? Could you pass the blind taste test?
